rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydrogenation of unactivated alkyl alkenes using catalytic chlorosilanes as a Si-based H-carrier with H2O as the terminal hydrogen source, mediated by Fe (sacrificial anode) and Ni cocatalysis.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Ph3SiCl to a silyl anion (via silyl radical) that abstracts H from H2O to give Ph3SiH; concurrently the sacrificial Fe anode releases Fe(II/III) salts that enter an Fe-H catalytic cycle delivering H to alkene; Ni(II) precatalyst (NiBr2·dtbpy) provides a parallel Ni-H pathway with opposite regioselectivity.
electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ph3SiCl (via Si radical to silyl anion) which is protonated by H2O to give Ph3SiH; Ph3SiH transfers H to an Fe(II)-X species generated from the sacrificial Fe anode, forming Fe-H that inserts into the alkene; hydrolysis of the resulting Fe-alkyl by H2O releases alkane and Fe-OH, which is reduced back into the cycle, while Ph3SiOH (resting Si species) is recycled via fluorination/chlorination back to Ph3SiX.

probe_reagents.name: D2O / CF3CH2OD
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterium incorporation in product confirms H2O/ROH is the H-source; inverse D-ratio between Fe-H and Ni-H regimes supports two parallel metal-hydride cycles.

probe_reagents.name: CF3CH2OH (replacing H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Ph3SiOCH2CF3 detected by 19F NMR, confirming silanol/silyl-ether intermediates; reaction still proceeds in 90% yield.

probe_reagents.name: (TMS)2O
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows E1/2 = -3.17 V vs Ag/Ag+; under electrolysis converts to TMSOCH2CF3, supporting Si-O cleavage and re-entry to Si cycle.

probe_reagents.name: GC-MS detection of Ph3SiOH
probe_reagents.observation: Significant Ph3SiOH peak at 15.6 min after 5 h electrolysis, identifying silanol as the stable Si resting species.

probe_reagents.name: PhMe2SiCl (1.0-2.0 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Disilane Ph(Me)2Si-Si(Me)2Ph isolated (56-58%) alongside hydrogenation product (15-20%), evidencing silyl-anion intermediate via nucleophilic Si-Si coupling.

probe_reagents.name: Fe(BF4)2·6H2O / FeCl3 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric mixing with Ph3SiH and alkene gave 0% alkane (98% recovered SM), showing that electrochemistry is indispensable for generating the active Fe species.

probe_reagents.name: H2O loading screen (0, 1, 2, 3, 5 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: Sharply non-monotonic: 0 equiv 92% (H from DMF), 1/2/5 equiv 0%, 3 equiv 92%; consistent with a narrow optimum balancing H-transfer vs. silane hydrolysis.

notes: Authors favor a homogeneous dual Fe/Ni-hydride cycle with a Si-shuttle (Ph3SiCl -> Ph3Si- -> Ph3SiH -> Ph3SiOH -> back to Ph3SiX). Reviewers raised serious alternatives: (i) heterogeneous Fe/Ni nanoparticle hydrogenation from electrodeposited Fe on Ni foam, and (ii) hydroxysilane (Ph3SiOH) rather than chlorosilane being the true active mediator. The stoichiometric Fe(BF4)2/FeCl3 + Ph3SiH controls gave no alkane, which the authors attribute to inability to mimic the anodically generated Fe species but reviewers argue is consistent with heterogeneous catalysis. Ni-H and Fe-H proposed to give opposite regioselectivity (branch vs linear), supported by DFT (Figures S2/S3). Disilane formation at high chlorosilane loading is rationalized as off-pathway Si-Si coupling of silyl anion with chlorosilane.
